stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of body weight using a bathroom scale. The measurements have a mean of 28.0 kg and a standard deviation of 2.7 kg. However, calibration tests reveal that a bathroom scale systematically underestimates the true value by between 3.1% and 7.6%. Estimate the true body weight and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 28.0 kg. After correcting for the 5.3% bias: ≈ 29.6 kg. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.1%-7.6%) and sampling variability: [27.5, 31.7]. Point estimate: 29.6 kg.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
